title: Brachiaria notochthona
intro: Brachiaria notochthona, also known as Brachiaria, is a type of tropical grass used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Bioactive constituents of Brachiaria notochthona include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Brachiaria is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, with traditional uses including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Brachiaria notochthona belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It is further classified under the subfamily Panicoideae. Within this subfamily, it is placed in the tribe Paniceae. The genus Brachiaria is characterized by its small, delicate flowers and a wide range of species, many of which are found in tropical regions. As a member of the Brachiaria genus, Brachiaria notochthona is closely related to other species within this group.
